components.assessment: Complete CMS coverage, checksum, exact identity, and deterministic artifacts validate.
Broad categories are coherent, with concentrated uncertainty in prolonged injury and device contexts.
Override recovery exists, but the targeted review queue is material.
Only mapped PASS records have stable reusable content identity.
IDs are deterministic, but major under-fragmentation and sparse family mapping remain.

failCategories.label: Fractures
failCategories.reason: Most active fracture episodes are not CCM-specific, but delayed healing, nonunion, malunion, and functional consequences merit review.

examples.rationale: The code represents active treatment or routine follow-up of an acute injury, poisoning, or external-cause event.

mergeOpportunities.recommendation: Consider one chronic-pain canonical core with back-pain and neoplasm-related variants; preserve site-specific functional modules where needed.
Retain distinct canonical IDs, but share a diabetes core and complication modules to avoid duplicate questions.
Keep neuropathy independently selectable while reusing a complication module for diabetic neuropathy.

splitOpportunities.recommendation: Split general content by uncomplicated disease, ulceration, gangrene, and revascularization/amputation burden; laterality alone should still collapse.
Retain site families but add active treatment, metastatic disease, remission/surveillance, and symptom-burden dimensions.
Separate general inflammatory arthritis from organ involvement, treatment toxicity, and severe functional impairment modules.
Current-fracture separation is useful; consider healing status and recurrent-fracture risk without creating site-by-side profiles.
Add deficit-specific modules for cognition, speech, motor impairment, swallowing, and mobility while sharing a stroke core.
Laterality should remain collapsed, but weight-bearing versus upper-extremity disease may need different function and fall-risk modules.
